description: Adversarial invariant for the manual medical-imaging sample audit: CT, MRI, ultrasound, and electron microscopy must stay separated by physical method and reconstruction dependency.

checks.reason: MRI is NMR-based and must not depend on the removed CT/MRI bundle.
NMR is the hard physical foundation for MRI.
Hospital information systems are clinical workflow infrastructure, not MRI method prerequisites.
Ultrasound is acoustic imaging and must not depend on X-ray imaging.
Medical ultrasound needs ultrasonic transduction to generate and receive sound pulses.
Hospital information systems should not be a direct modality prerequisite for ultrasound.
Electron microscopy reconstruction should not depend on a medical CT/MRI bundle.
Computational reconstruction algorithms are the relevant advanced electron microscopy enabler.
Medical CT reconstructs images from X-ray measurements.
Computer reconstruction is an enabling dependency for practical medical CT.
The future brain-mapping node should use the concrete MRI node rather than a removed CT/MRI bundle.
Standalone PET should not depend directly on CT; PET/CT would be a separate hybrid modality.
PET should keep radionuclide-production context instead of a CT prerequisite.
Piezoelectricity should stay anchored to the Curie discovery date.
Ultrasonic transducers should stay earlier than medical ultrasound.
Medical ultrasound should not regress to a generated postwar placeholder date.
NMR should remain earlier than MRI.
CT should stay anchored to the early clinical scanner chronology, not a 1958 generated date.
MRI should stay anchored to the Lauterbur/Mansfield imaging chronology.
